twitter_thread: I tested 50 productivity apps and found 45 were clones. Only 5 innovated.
RescueTime tracks 4,000+ apps, but Harvest also tracks $1.2M in expenses for a typical business.
Since switching to 90-minute work cycles, I've seen a 25% boost in code output.
Evernote's 100MB note limit is crippling. Simplenote handles 10x that size.
Jira and Asana overlap on 80% of features. Pick one to avoid tool fatigue.
What hidden feature in your favorite app do you wish more people knew? #productivityhacks #software
pack.linkedin: After testing 50 productivity apps, I found only 5 with truly unique features. For example, RescueTime tracks time spent on 4,000+ apps, but Harvest also tracks $1.2M in expenses for a typical business. I've also seen a 25% boost in code output since switching to 90-minute work cycles. Simplenote handles notes 10x larger than Evernote's 100MB limit. What's your go-to productivity app, and how do you use it to maximize your workflow?
tiktok_hooks: One tweak to your daily routine can save 2 hours of busywork.
I deleted 17 productivity apps and my workflow improved.
The $0 productivity hack that's 5x more effective than any paid tool.
pack.reddit_headline: Can you name 3 productivity apps that genuinely improved your workflow?
